title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
A detective delivers a telegram from the Chief of Police in Belgrade, Yugoslavia, to Vane and the investigators aboard the ship. The telegram details an ancient feud between the Tveril and Croscak families: Stephen, Andrew, and Timslof Tveril murdered Vega Croscak's father and two uncles before robbing the estate and fleeing to Montenegro. Ellery notes that this history provides a motive for Vega Crocetta to murder the Tveril brothers to avenge his family. Additionally, a detective brings reports regarding the Rein family, though sightings of them in Arizona and Florida remain unconfirmed.

A detective named Bill reports finding evidence near the dock, including a rowboat with a rope cut by a sharp knife and five footprints in the mud. Bill observes that the footprints belong to a man with an eight-and-a-half-inch foot who walks with a heavy limp, as his right heel sinks much deeper into the mud than his left.

Meanwhile, Elias Darling, a shopkeeper from Huntington, is brought aboard by police. Darling testifies that he saw a short man and a young woman park a Buick sedan in front of his store. Later, a tall, broad-shouldered man carrying a small travel bag opened the unlocked car, started the engine, and drove toward Central Harbor. Detective Pickard clarifies that a couple reported that specific Buick stolen in Huntington at two o'clock that morning. Ellery suggests that the suspect, Croscot, likely stole the car to facilitate his escape and then abandoned it.
